# SL-META-005: missing-description

<!-- Generated by scripts/generate-rule-docs.mjs. Do not edit by hand. -->

## Summary

A route requiring a meta description has none.

## Metadata

- Category: Title and metadata (title-metadata)
- Default severity: error
- Confidence: certain
- Scope: page
- Provider scope: core
- Version: 1.0.0

## Evidence Sources

- raw-html
- rendered-dom
- source-code

## Required Evidence

- route-contract
- description-state

## When This Triggers

Resolve description requirement and inspect source, raw HTML, and rendered DOM.

## False Positives

False positives are expected to be rare because this rule relies on
deterministic local evidence. Suppress only with a reason when the route
contract intentionally differs from the observed signal.

## How To Fix

Define a server-rendered meta description.

## Examples

Bad-case fixture reference:

- fixtures/meta/missing-description

Fixed direction:

- Define a server-rendered meta description.

The generated documentation does not invent HTML, DSL, source locations,
selectors, or external observations beyond the catalog fixture references above.

## Implementation Surfaces

- Next.js/source analyzer can provide source-code evidence when the relevant
  files are supplied.
- CLI, crawler, browser collector, overlay, and Next.js dev mode can share this
  rule through the common core engine.
